🏆 Leaderboard
Loading…
This board covers the current prize period set by your teacher, and is scored differently from your Mastery level, so it can't be gamed by re-answering old mistakes.
  • Correct on your first try at a question this period = 1.0 point.
  • Got it wrong first, then correct on retry = 0.3 points instead of 1.0.
  • Only your first 2 retries of the same question count toward your score each period — further retries on that question this period earn 0, though you can keep practicing them for your own learning.
  • Each question can only contribute points once per period, on whichever of your counted attempts was correct.
  • Completing a task your teacher assigned can add a one-time bonus on top — but only if your teacher checked "Count this task toward leaderboards" when assigning it, and only if your accuracy on that task met the required minimum. The bonus size scales with the number of questions in the task and your accuracy on it.
  • Answering your Daily Warm-Up correctly adds a flat +10 point bonus to this board — once per day, the day it's completed.
Ties are broken by first-try accuracy %, then by number of distinct questions attempted.

👥 Study Groups
Team up with up to 2 classmates near your level. Any member can invite more teammates or start a new group, and any member can send a lock request once you hit 3 — but the group only becomes permanent once your teacher confirms it. Invites are matched by level — see "How do levels work?" on the Leaderboard.
Group score = for each member, add up a value for every question in the bank based on how they did on it, then add all members' totals together. Per question, per member: 1 point if correct on the first attempt, 0.5 points if wrong first then fixed on the very next redo, and 0 points otherwise. Only the first attempt and one redo count — trying a question more times after that doesn't change its value.
How to raise your group's score:
  • Answering a brand-new question correctly on your first try is worth the most — 1 full point.
  • Going back and fixing a question you got wrong is worth 0.5 points, and only your first redo of it counts — extra retries after that don't add more.
  • Getting a question wrong that you'd previously gotten right can lower its value, since it's re-regraded against your most recent tries at it.
  • Recruiting active members matters too — a member who's never attempted anything contributes 0, so a smaller but genuinely improving group can outscore a larger, less active one.
Group members can see each other's individual contribution inside "My Group" — the public Group Leaderboard only shows group totals, never individual scores.

🛠 Turn On Maintenance Mode
Students will be locked out of the platform right after logging in or signing up, until you turn this back off.
How long should it stay on?
Exceptions
Selected accounts can keep using the platform normally, unaffected by maintenance mode.
Loading users…
Notice for students (optional)
🛠 Maintenance Mode
When maintenance mode is on, students can still log in or sign up as normal, but instead of the platform they'll only see a notice that the platform is under maintenance — until you turn it back off.
🗂️ Category Maintenance
Temporarily disable one or more categories — e.g. while you're editing their questions — without locking out the whole platform. Students still see the category, but it's greyed out and unclickable, and they can't Battle-challenge anyone on it either, until you turn it back off.
🗂️ Category Maintenance
Categories listed here are currently locked — invisible to click, greyed out, and unusable everywhere (Quiz, Test, Battle, Redo My Mistakes) for students, until you turn each one off or its timer runs out. You can grant individual students an exception so they can still practice (without saving) — see "Exceptions" on each locked category below.
🧑‍🎓 Exceptions
Students you check here can still practice this category's questions while it's locked — they see and attempt everything normally, they just won't have any attempt saved until you unlock it. Everyone else stays fully locked out, exactly as now.

❓ Help
Tap any feature below to see how it works.
This is where you actually answer questions. Pick a category, choose a mode, and start:
  • Practice mode — no pressure. You get told right away if you're right or wrong, and you can see explanations as you go. Best for learning.
  • Quiz mode — a short, timed set of questions, feedback shown after you finish.
  • Test mode — feels like a real exam. Answer everything first, then see your results at the end.
Every question you answer — right or wrong — gets remembered. That history is what powers everything else on this platform: your level, your leaderboard scores, your milestones, all of it.
A shortcut that gathers every question you've gotten wrong and lets you try them again. Since your score on a question is based on your average across all your attempts, getting it right this time genuinely improves your standing — it's not just for show.
Lets you race against a recording of your own past performance — same questions, same order — so you can see directly whether you've actually improved since last time.
Tap "Leaderboard" and you'll see two tabs. They are not the same thing and are scored completely differently on purpose.
📈 Mastery Board (your Level, L1–L10)
This measures how much of the entire question bank you've mastered overall — a long-term, permanent record. The more questions you attempt (and the better your track record on each one), the higher your level climbs. This one never resets — it's built from your whole history. Your level also decides who you're matched against in Battle Mode and who you can invite to a Study Group.
💰 Weekly Prize Board
This is a short-term competition that resets on a schedule your teacher sets (e.g. every week). It rewards getting things right the first time — you get full points for a first-try correct answer, much less for getting it right only after a couple of retries, and nothing at all if you need more than a couple of tries. This stops people from just guessing repeatedly until they get it right. It's meant to reflect how sharp you are right now, not your whole history.
Challenge a classmate to a head-to-head set of questions — you choose the category, how many questions (not fixed, you pick the number), and a time limit when you create the challenge. You don't need to be online at the same time as your opponent. You're only matched against people close to your own level, so it stays fair.
Team up with up to 2 classmates near your level to form a group. Anyone can start a group and become its leader, and any member — not just the leader — can invite more teammates. Once your group reaches 3 people, any member can send a lock request; your teacher then reviews it and confirms the lock, which makes membership permanent for good — so choose your teammates carefully. Your group has its own leaderboard too, based on everyone's combined effort.
Sometimes your teacher will assign you a specific task — a set of questions to complete, sometimes with a deadline and a minimum accuracy requirement. You'll see these under "Tasks."
Completing a task always gets marked as done. But some tasks also have a bonus attached — your teacher decides this when creating the task:
  • If your teacher turned on leaderboard bonuses for a task, and you hit the required accuracy, finishing it gives you extra points on the Weekly Prize board and a small extra boost to your Mastery level — on top of whatever you'd normally earn just from answering those questions.
  • If a task doesn't have that bonus turned on, finishing it is still tracked and shown as complete — it just doesn't add anything extra to either leaderboard.
If a teacher deletes a task later, any extra bonus that task gave you is automatically taken back out of both leaderboards.
A collection of achievements you unlock as you go — things like streaks (practicing several days in a row), hitting certain accuracy marks, or reaching a new level. Purely a way to see your progress, doesn't affect scoring.
Counts how many days in a row you've practiced. Keeping it alive is its own kind of milestone.
